meta.title: Lumetrix | Sidhilynx Python Programming Question Bank

test_case_structure.note: Test cases to be filled per question

questions.question: Write a Python program to find the largest of three numbers entered by a user.
Write a program that checks whether a given number is even or odd and prints the result.
Create a program that adds two numbers 15 and 27, then subtracts 10 from the result and displays the final output.
Write a program that reads a number from the user and prints "Positive" if the number is greater than 0, otherwise prints "Non-positive".
A student scores 78, 82, and 90 in three subjects. Write a program to calculate and display the average score. Moderate (Section A)
Write a program that reads a student's marks in 4 subjects, calculates the total, and assigns a grade: Total ≥ 300: Grade A | Total ≥ 240: Grade B | Total ≥ 180: Grade C | Total < 180: Grade D.
Create a program to find the factorial of a number N using a loop. Test with N=5 and N=7.
Write a program that reads marks of 30 students and counts how many students scored above 75.
Create a program to calculate the area of a circle. The program should read the radius from the user and use π = 3.14159.
Write a program to check if a year entered by the user is a leap year. (Hint: divisible by 4 but not by 100, OR divisible by 400)

Write a program that reads a student's attendance percentage and decides: ≥ 75%: LUMETRIX | SIDHILYNX Python Programming — Question Bank © 2026 Sidhilynx | Lumetrix Platform | Confidential | Page 6 "Eligible for exam" | < 75%: "Not eligible - Low attendance" Hard (Section A)
Create a program to find the GCD (Greatest Common Divisor) of two numbers using the Euclidean algorithm. Test with inputs 48 and 18.
A coffee vending machine charges ₹20 per cup. Write a program that takes the number of cups and calculates the total bill.
Create an ATM withdrawal program that checks if balance is sufficient. If balance ≥ withdrawal amount, allow withdrawal and show new balance, otherwise deny.
A parking lot charges ₹30 for the first hour and ₹20 for each additional hour. Write a program to calculate parking fees for H hours entered by user.
Create a simple calculator program that takes two numbers and an operator (+, -, *, /) and performs the operation.
A shop offers 10% discount if purchase amount > ₹1000. Write a program to calculate final payable amount.
Write a program that converts currency from INR to USD using exchange rate 1 USD = ₹83.
A fitness tracker counts steps. Write a program that reads daily steps for 7 days and calculates the weekly total and daily average. Moderate (Section B)
Create an e-commerce shipping calculator: Order < ₹500: ₹50 shipping | Order ₹500- ₹1000: ₹30 shipping | Order > ₹1000: Free shipping.
Write a temperature monitoring program that reads 24 hourly temperature readings and counts how many times temperature exceeded 35°C. Also find the maximum temperature recorded.
Create a movie ticket booking system: Weekday ₹150/ticket | Weekend ₹200/ticket | Senior citizens (age ≥ 60): 20% discount.
Write a program that validates whether a mobile number is correct: Must be exactly 10 digits | First digit must be 6, 7, 8, or 9.
Write a program to calculate electricity bill: 0-100 units ₹5/unit | 101-300 units ₹7/unit | 301+ units ₹10/unit. Also add ₹50 as fixed meter charge. Hard (Section B)

Write a program to find the maximum profit from stock prices. Given a list of 7 daily prices, find the best day to buy and best day to sell to maximize profit. Example: [100, 95, 110, 90, 105, 115, 85] → Buy on day 4 (₹90), Sell on day 6 (₹115), Profit = ₹25.

Create a password strength checker that evaluates a password in ONE pass: Check for uppercase letter, lowercase letter, digit, special character | Length ≥ 8 | Print strength: Weak (0-2 criteria), Medium (3 criteria), Strong (4+ criteria). LUMETRIX | SIDHILYNX Python Programming — Question Bank © 2026 Sidhilynx | Lumetrix Platform | Confidential | Page 7
Write a program that demonstrates the use of all basic data types in Python: int, float, str, bool. Create one variable of each type and print them with their types using type().
Create a program that takes a student's name and roll number as input, then displays them in the format: "Student Name: [name], Roll No: [number]"
Write a program that declares variables for length and breadth of a rectangle, calculates area and perimeter, and displays the results with appropriate labels.
Create a program that takes three subject marks as input (float values) and prints them rounded to 2 decimal places.
Write a program that demonstrates the difference between print() statements with and without the sep and end parameters.
Write a program that takes a sentence as input and prints: the sentence in uppercase | the sentence in lowercase | the length of the sentence.
Create a program that demonstrates variable reassignment. Start with x = 10, then x = 20, then x = "Hello". Print value and type after each assignment. Moderate (Section A)
Write a program that takes radius as input, calculates both area and circumference of a circle, and displays results formatted to 3 decimal places.
Create a program that demonstrates type conversion: Take a float input (45.67), convert to int | Take an int input (100), convert to float and string.
Write a program that takes marks in 5 subjects as input, calculates total and percentage, then prints a formatted marksheet with Subject-wise marks, Total, and Percentage.
Create a program that reads a 4-digit number and separates it into individual digits using integer division and modulo operations.
Write a program that takes base and height of a triangle as float inputs, calculates area, and handles the output formatting to show exactly 2 decimal places.

Write a program that reads temperature in Celsius as float, converts to Fahrenheit and Kelvin, and displays all three temperatures in a formatted table structure. LUMETRIX | SIDHILYNX Python Programming — Question Bank © 2026 Sidhilynx | Lumetrix Platform | Confidential | Page 8
Create a program that takes a 6-digit student ID number as input and extracts: First 2 digits: Year of admission | Next 2 digits: Department code | Last 2 digits: Roll number. Hard (Section A)

Write a program that validates user input for age: Keep asking for age until a valid integer is entered | Display "Invalid input" for non-numeric input | Check if age is between 1 and 120 | Use try- except for error handling.

Create a program that demonstrates the limitations of float data type: Add 0.1 + 0.2 and print result | Check if result equals 0.3 | Show how to properly compare floats using round() or checking difference.
Write a program that takes a large number (e.g., 1234567890) and formats it with commas as thousand separators, in scientific notation, and as a string with custom formatting using f-strings.
Create a simple invoice generator that takes product name, quantity, and price per unit as input, then displays Product, Quantity, Price per unit, and Total.
Write a program for a digital weighing scale that takes weight in kilograms (float) and converts it to Grams and Pounds (1 kg = 2.20462 lbs).
Create a program that takes customer name and phone number as input, then displays a confirmation message.
Write a currency denomination counter: Take an amount in rupees (integer), then calculate how many ₹500, ₹100, ₹50, ₹20, ₹10 notes are needed.
Create a simple time converter that takes time in minutes and converts it to hours and minutes format. Example: Input 135 → Output "2 hours 15 minutes"
Write a program for a fuel efficiency calculator: Input distance traveled (km) and fuel consumed (liters) | Output: Mileage in km/l formatted to 2 decimal places.
Create a simple tip calculator: Input bill amount | Calculate 10%, 15%, and 20% tip options | Display all three options.
Write a program for a juice shop that takes number of glasses sold and displays: Small (₹30), Medium (₹50), Large (₹70) totals and Total Revenue. Moderate (Section B)
Create a BMI calculator with formatted output: Input name, weight (kg), height (m) | Calculate BMI | Display a personalized report with Name, Weight, Height, and BMI.
Write a program for an online shopping cart: Input product name, original price, discount percentage | Calculate discount amount and final price | Display all details formatted with ₹ symbol.
Create a gym membership calculator: Input member name, membership type (monthly/quarterly/yearly) | Rates: Monthly ₹1000, Quarterly ₹2700 (10% off), Yearly ₹9600 (20% off).

Write a program that validates and formats phone numbers: Input 10-digit number | Check if exactly 10 digits | Format as +91-XXXXX-XXXXX. LUMETRIX | SIDHILYNX Python Programming — Question Bank © 2026 Sidhilynx | Lumetrix Platform | Confidential | Page 9
Create an age calculator: Input birth year | Calculate current age (use 2024 as current year) | Calculate age in months and days (approximate).
Write a comprehensive salary slip generator: Input employee name, employee ID, basic salary | Calculate HRA (40%), DA (20%), PF (12%) | Display formatted salary slip.
Create a scientific calculator for quadratic equation: Input a, b, c in ax² + bx + c | Calculate discriminant | Show nature of roots | Handle a = 0 case.

Write a program for a hotel booking system: Input guest name, room number, nights, room rate, is_member (bool) | Members get 15% discount | Add 18% GST | Display complete booking confirmation. LUMETRIX | SIDHILYNX Python Programming — Question Bank © 2026 Sidhilynx | Lumetrix Platform | Confidential | Page 10
Write a program that takes two numbers and demonstrates all arithmetic operators (+, -, *, /, //, %, **). Display each operation with its result.
Create a program that takes a number and prints its square, cube, and square root using appropriate operators.
Write a program that takes two integers and compares them using all relational operators (>, <, >=, <=, ==, !=). Print True/False for each comparison.
Create a program that demonstrates the difference between / (division) and // (floor division) with the same pair of numbers. Test with 17 and 5.
Write a program that takes a number and checks if it's divisible by both 3 and 5 using the modulo operator.
Create a program using compound assignment operators (+=, -=, *=, /=). Start with x = 10, perform all operations.
Write a program that takes a number and checks if it's positive, negative, or zero using relational operators. Moderate (Section A)
Write a program that takes coefficients a, b, c of a quadratic equation and calculates the discriminant (b² - 4ac). Use this to determine if roots are real, equal, or imaginary.
Create a program that takes a 4-digit number and reverses it using only arithmetic operators (no string conversion).
Write a program that takes exam marks and attendance percentage, then checks eligibility: (marks >= 40) and (attendance >= 75). Print detailed reason if not eligible.
Create a program demonstrating operator chaining in comparisons: Check if 10 < x < 100 for a given x.
Write a program that takes a student's score (0-100) and uses relational operators to check: Excellent (>=90), Good (>=75), Average (>=50), Fail (<50).

Create a program that evaluates De Morgan's Laws: Verify not(A and B) == (not A) or (not B) and not(A or B) == (not A) and (not B). Take boolean inputs for A and B. LUMETRIX | SIDHILYNX Python Programming — Question Bank © 2026 Sidhilynx | Lumetrix Platform | Confidential | Page 11
Write a program that takes total seconds and converts to hours:minutes:seconds format using only //, % operators. Hard (Section A)
Create a program that solves a quadratic equation ax² + bx + c = 0 completely: Calculate discriminant | Find both roots | Handle all cases (real distinct, real equal, complex).
Write a program that evaluates operator precedence in complex expressions: 2 + 3 * 4 ** 2 / 8 - 5 % 3 | Show step-by-step evaluation.

Write a program that checks multiple conditions for loan approval: Age between 21-60 | Income > 25000 OR co-applicant income > 15000 | Credit score >= 700 | Existing loans < 3 | Combine all using logical operators.
Write a program for a retail shop that calculates: Subtotal (price * quantity) | Tax (subtotal * 0.18) | Total (subtotal + tax).
Create a program that checks if a customer is eligible for a discount: Purchase amount >= 1000. Use relational operators.
Write a program for a pizza delivery system that adds ₹40 delivery charge if order amount < 300, otherwise free delivery.
Create a program that calculates electricity bill: Units consumed * ₹7 + ₹50 (fixed charge).
Write a program that checks if a user's age is valid for driving license (age >= 18). Use relational operators.
Create a program for a taxi service: Base fare ₹50 + ₹12 * distance_km. Calculate total fare.
Write a program that calculates discount amount: Original price * (discount_percentage / 100). Moderate (Section B)
Write a program for an e-commerce site that calculates final price: Apply discount if amount > 1000 | Add shipping if amount < 500 | Add GST (18%).
Create a program for credit card payment: Minimum payment = 5% of outstanding OR ₹500 (whichever is higher). Calculate both, use relational operators to select maximum.
Write a program for insurance premium calculation: Base premium ₹10000 | Add 20% if age < 25 OR age > 60 | Add 15% if smoker | Subtract 10% if regular_exercise.
Create a program for a telecom billing system: Free up to 100 minutes | ₹1 per minute for 101-300 minutes | ₹0.50 per minute beyond 300.
Write a program for overtime pay calculation: Regular hours (<=40): rate * hours | Overtime hours (>40): rate * 1.5 * extra_hours | Weekend hours: rate * 2 * weekend_hours.

Create a program for traffic fine calculation: Base fine ₹500 | Add ₹100 for every 10 km/h above limit | Double fine if school zone | Triple fine if repeat offender. Write a program for dynamic pricing algorithm: Base price | Demand factor (high/medium/low: +30%/0%/-20%) | Inventory factor (low stock: +15%, high stock: -10%) | Member: -10%, New customer: +5%.

Create a program for a stock trading profit/loss calculator: Buy price, sell price, quantity, brokerage (0.5%) | Short term capital gains tax (15%) if held < 1 year | Long term (10%) on profit > ₹100000 if held >= 1 year. LUMETRIX | SIDHILYNX Python Programming — Question Bank © 2026 Sidhilynx | Lumetrix Platform | Confidential | Page 32 CAPSTONE CHALLENGES The following capstone projects are designed for extended sessions (60-120 minutes). They integrate multiple modules and require architectural thinking, not just coding. Capstone 1: Complete Banking System Modules: Modules 2, 5, 6, 8, 9 Build a complete banking system that manages multiple account types (savings, current, fixed deposit). Implement: customer registration with validation, deposit/withdrawal with daily limits, fund transfers between accounts, interest calculation (simple and compound), loan eligibility checker, EMI calculator, fraud detection (flag suspicious transactions), generate monthly statement. Use functions, loops, lists for account storage, dictionaries for transactions, file handling for persistence, and custom exceptions for error management. Capstone 2: Student Management & Examination System Modules: Modules 2, 4, 5, 6, 7, 8 Build a complete student management system: Student registration with ID generation, course enrollment with prerequisite checking, attendance tracking (flag < 75%), marks entry for multiple subjects with validation (0-100), automated grade calculation (A+/A/B+/B/C/F), grace marks policy (failed by <=5 marks AND overall > 45%), result processing (Distinction/First Class/Second Class/Pass/Fail), generate formatted report cards, rank list generation, scholarship eligibility checker. Use nested lists for data storage, functions for each module, file handling for data persistence. Capstone 3: E-Commerce Platform Modules: Modules 4, 5, 6, 7, 8, 9 Build a mini e-commerce platform: Product catalog management (add/remove/update products using dictionaries), shopping cart (add items, update quantity, remove), dynamic pricing engine (base price + demand factor + inventory factor + member discount), coupon code validation, checkout process (apply discounts → calculate tax → add shipping → final bill), inventory management with auto-reorder alerts, order history tracking, product recommendations based on purchase history using sets, generate itemized invoice. Handle all edge cases with proper exception handling. Capstone 4: Hospital Management System Modules: Modules 4, 5, 6, 8, 9, 10 Build a hospital management system: Patient registration with unique ID, appointment booking (check doctor availability, avoid conflicts using sets), queue management for different departments (emergency priority), doctor schedule management, prescription tracking with drug interaction checking (using tuple database), bed allocation system (2D list for floor/room), billing system (room charges + doctor fees + medicines + tests, insurance coverage), generate patient bill and discharge summary. Store data in files, use custom exceptions for medical errors. LUMETRIX | SIDHILYNX Python Programming — Question Bank © 2026 Sidhilynx | Lumetrix Platform | Confidential | Page 33 Capstone 5: Inventory & Supply Chain System Modules: Modules 5, 6, 8, 9, 10 Build an inventory and supply chain management system: Multi-warehouse stock tracking (nested dictionaries with location: {product: quantity}), supplier management, automatic purchase order generation when stock < reorder level, goods receipt processing, stock transfer between warehouses, FIFO inventory valuation, ABC analysis (A: top 20% value, B: next 30%, C: remaining 50%), demand forecasting using moving averages, generate inventory valuation report and stock movement log. Import/export data from CSV files, handle all file and data exceptions. Capstone 6: Real Estate Management Platform Modules: Modules 4, 6, 7, 8, 9, 10 Build a real estate platform: Property listing management (residential/commercial/land) with nested dictionaries, property search with multiple filters (location, type, price range, area), EMI calculator for home loans with complete amortization schedule, rental management (rent tracking, lease agreements, maintenance requests), agent commission calculator (tiered: 2% first 50L, 1.5% next 50L, 1% above 1Cr + GST 18% + agent/broker split), property valuation using multiple models, generate property report and transaction history. Store all data in JSON files. Capstone 7: HR & Payroll Processing System Modules: Modules 4, 5, 6, 8, 9, 10 Build a complete HR and payroll system: Employee database management (personal info, department, designation, salary grade), attendance tracking with leave management (casual/sick/earned leave with carry-forward rules), salary calculation engine (basic + HRA 40% + DA 20% + TA + special allowances - PF 12% - ESI - professional tax - TDS based on income slab), performance review system with rating- based increment calculation, bonus calculation (festival bonus + performance bonus), generate payslip and Form 16 equivalent. Multi-file data management with proper exception handling. Capstone 8: Tournament Management System Modules: Modules 4, 5, 6, 7, 8 Build a complete sports tournament manager: Support multiple formats (round-robin, knockout, group stage + knockout), team registration with player roster management, fixture generation avoiding scheduling conflicts, match result entry and validation, points table calculation (wins: 3pts, draw: 1pt, loss: 0pts) with tiebreaker rules (goal difference, head-to-head), progression to knockout rounds based on standing, player statistics tracking (goals, assists, yellow/red cards), generate match schedule and standings table. Handle all edge cases (walkovers, abandoned matches, etc.). Capstone 9: Financial Portfolio Analyzer Modules: Modules 3, 5, 6, 8, 9, 10 Build a financial portfolio analyzer: Multi-asset portfolio management (stocks, bonds, gold, FD, mutual funds), historical price tracking with moving averages (10-day, 50-day, 200-day), portfolio performance metrics (absolute return, CAGR, Sharpe ratio concept), risk assessment (volatility, beta), tax calculation on gains (STCG 15% if < 1 year for equity, LTCG 10% on profit > ₹1L if >= 1 year), dividend tracking, rebalancing recommendations when allocation deviates from target, generate comprehensive portfolio report. Load/save portfolio data from JSON files. LUMETRIX | SIDHILYNX Python Programming — Question Bank © 2026 Sidhilynx | Lumetrix Platform | Confidential | Page 34 Capstone 10: Smart Restaurant System Modules: Modules 4, 5, 6, 8, 9, 10 Build a complete smart restaurant management system: Menu management with categories and dynamic pricing (happy hours -30%, peak hours +20%, weekend +15%, festival +25%), table booking system with time slot management (avoid conflicts using sets), order management per table (add items, modify, cancel), kitchen queue optimization (priority by order time and table priority), bill generation (food + GST 5% + service charge 10% + applicable discounts - loyalty points), split bill option, daily sales analytics (revenue, popular items, peak hours, table turnover), inventory depletion tracking per order. Full file-based data persistence. LUMETRIX | SIDHILYNX Python Programming — Question Bank © 2026 Sidhilynx | Lumetrix Platform | Confidential | Page 35 Total Questions: 374 Empowering Learners.
